verdict: High risk of AI displacement due to routine_cognitive and ai_capability_gap scores, but mitigated by regulatory_moat and physical_dexterity dimensions.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition is high
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like collecting body tissue, feces, blood, urine, or other body fluids for examination and analysis (task 4) are routine pattern-matching on text/data that current frontier LLMs can handle.

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap is significant
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like operating diagnostic equipment, such as radiographic or ultrasound equipment, and interpreting the resulting images (task 5) are already being performed by AI systems with high accuracy.

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

recent_developments.headline: Hospitals deploy ambient AI scribes at scale
recent_developments.impact: Major US health systems report clinicians saving 1–2 hours of documentation per day.

recent_developments.headline: Drug discovery accelerates with foundation models
recent_developments.impact: AI-designed candidates are entering Phase I trials within months instead of years.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
